rationale: The cited paper is used as a key prior position in the core debate the article addresses: whether private labels create retailer differentiation and loyalty. The citing paper repeatedly frames its contribution against this view, asking whether PL benefits spill across chains and thus reduce chain-specific advantages. Its results (cross-retailer PL learning and dampened PL-vs-PL rivalry) qualify the earlier differentiation claim rather than merely listing the citation as background.
relation_description: The citing paper takes Corstjens and Lal's store-brand differentiation/loyalty argument as an important starting point, then adds a boundary condition via cross-brand learning across retailers' PLs. It shows that PL investments can spill over to rival PLs, reducing retailer-specific differentiation even as PLs gain against national brands.
